metadata.summary: Author Claude Skills as a three-level pyramid to defer context loading: frontmatter always loaded, body only on activation, references only on explicit Read.

content.title: Progressive Disclosure Skills — 3-Level Pyramid
content.summary: Author Claude Skills as a strict three-level pyramid: (1) YAML frontmatter — name + description + triggers, always loaded into the system prompt; (2) SKILL.md body — loaded only when the skill is activated by trigger match; (3) scripts/, references/, assets/ — loaded only when the activated SKILL.md explicitly tells Claude to read them. Each level pays a context cost only at activation, not by default.

why.p: Anthropic's Skills system was built specifically to solve "the import everything anti-pattern" — agents that pre-load all instructions into the system prompt blow context budgets and degrade reasoning quality. Progressive disclosure mirrors how a senior engineer reads a codebase: scan the index, open the chapter that matches the task, follow only the references that are actually relevant. Empirically, Claude's tool-selection accuracy holds well past 25 skills when each one obeys this rule, and collapses past ~10 when bodies are inlined into the system prompt.

list.item: Authoring any reusable capability whose instructions exceed ~100 lines.
Domain-specific workflows reused across multiple projects (deploy procedures, commit conventions, design-review rules).
Building a Claude Code agent that ships with several skills and must keep base context under ~5k tokens.
Migrating a long CLAUDE.md into modular skills as project complexity grows.
One-off instructions that apply to a single session — inline into CLAUDE.md instead.
Logic that should run as code, not text — package as an MCP tool.
Tiny rules under ~20 lines — skill machinery overhead exceeds the win.
Workflows where the "skill" would actually be a prompt template — use prompts/ in MCP, not Skills.

section.name: Three loading levels of a Claude Skill
section.p: A Skill is a pyramid: frontmatter is always loaded, SKILL.md loads on activation, bundled files load only on explicit Read.

section.name: Level 1 — frontmatter (always loaded)
section.p: YAML frontmatter is the only part of a skill that always lives in the system prompt. Keep it tiny — Claude scans every skill's frontmatter on every request to decide what to activate.

rule.statement: Frontmatter must contain at minimum name, description, and trigger keywords; the description must be under 30 lines and front-load specific verbs and nouns the user would say.
rule.rationale: Anthropic's skill-router matches the user message against the description text; vague descriptions ("helps with stuff") trigger nothing and inflate base context for zero value.

example.code: --- name: faion-deploy description: Use when user asks to deploy a faion-net project (faion.net, dev.faion.net, api.faion.net, neromedia, pashtelka, longlife, ender). Handles op_unlock, deploy-gh.sh selection, post-deploy verification, telegram notification. ---
example.note: Description names every domain a user might mention, plus the steps it covers.

example.code: --- name: deployer description: A skill that helps with deployment. ---
example.note: No domain triggers, no steps, no project hints — router has nothing to match.

section.name: Level 2 — SKILL.md body (loaded on activation)
section.p: SKILL.md is loaded into the conversation only when Claude decides the skill applies. Keep it focused on procedure and decisions, defer everything else.

rule.statement: SKILL.md body must stay under 500 lines and must reference scripts, examples, and long reference docs by relative path rather than inlining their content.
rule.rationale: Activated body sits in context for the rest of the turn; inlining 2000-line reference docs costs that token tax even when the agent only needed 50 lines of it.

section.name: Level 3 — bundled files (loaded only on Read)
section.p: Anything in scripts/, references/, or assets/ loads only when SKILL.md tells Claude to Read it.

rule.statement: Place every artifact larger than ~80 lines (long runbooks, schemas, code samples) in a sibling folder and reference it from SKILL.md by relative path.
rule.rationale: Files are loaded lazily on Read — a 5000-line runbook in references/ costs zero context until the procedure actually needs it.

antipattern.description: Pasting an entire reference document inline as a fenced code block in SKILL.md.
antipattern.reason: Defeats progressive disclosure — the reference now loads on every activation, even for sub-tasks that never touch it.

section.name: Frontmatter authoring rules
section.p: Frontmatter must be short, trigger-rich, and concrete — the description IS the routing prompt.

rule.statement: Every SKILL.md frontmatter must declare name, description, and (when relevant) tier and user-invocable; missing description means the skill is invisible to the router.
rule.rationale: Claude's skill router only sees what the frontmatter exposes — fields it doesn't list cannot influence activation.

list.item: name — short, hyphenated, unique within the surface (e.g. faion-deploy).
description — first sentence "Use when ...", remaining sentences enumerate triggers and steps.
tier — free | solo | pro | geek (faion-network internal partition).
user-invocable — true if the user can call it directly via /name; false for auto-discovered skills.

section.name: Trigger-rich descriptions
section.p: The description is matched against the user's message; load it with verbs and nouns the user actually says.

rule.statement: Description must front-load action verbs (deploy, lint, generate, post, schedule) and concrete nouns (faion.net, neromedia, telegram, gatsby) instead of abstract category terms.
rule.rationale: Routers do soft string matching — "publish a new article to neromedia" reliably activates a description containing "neromedia" and "publish article", but not one that says "content workflows".

example.code: description: Use when user asks to publish, schedule, or queue a post to a NERO TG channel (neromedia, pashtelka, longlife, ender) or to inspect a posting pipeline. Wraps tg-send, post queue, classification.
example.note: Lists every channel name, every operation verb — wide surface for the router.

rule.statement: Frontmatter must stay under 30 non-blank lines; if the description exceeds this, split into multiple skills with narrower triggers.
rule.rationale: Frontmatter is in the system prompt for every request — every line costs across the entire session.

antipattern.description: Documenting the procedure in the description field instead of in SKILL.md body.
antipattern.reason: The procedure now loads on every request — defeats the entire point of leveling.

section.name: Progressive-disclosure anti-patterns
section.p: Three failure modes that collapse the pyramid into a flat blob: oversized body, missing triggers, and inlined references.

antipattern.description: SKILL.md grows past ~500 lines because the author keeps adding examples, edge cases, and runbooks inline.
antipattern.reason: Activated body sits in context for the rest of the turn; bloated bodies bust prompt cache, raise per-call cost, and crowd out tool results.

rule.statement: If SKILL.md exceeds 500 lines, split it: move detailed examples into references/<topic>.md and link them from the body with a one-line "Read references/X.md when ..." pointer.
rule.rationale: References load lazily — the agent reads them only when the linked condition triggers, keeping average context flat as the skill grows.

antipattern.description: Description is one sentence of marketing copy ("comprehensive deployment automation skill").
antipattern.reason: Router cannot match — the user says "deploy faion.net", description has neither word, skill never activates.

rule.statement: Every domain term, command name, or verb the user might say MUST appear in the description; aim for 3-6 concrete trigger phrases minimum.
rule.rationale: Skill activation is essentially semantic search over descriptions — coverage of trigger surface determines hit rate.

antipattern.description: Pasting a 1500-line runbook, schema, or sample dataset directly into a fenced code block in SKILL.md.
antipattern.reason: Inlined files load on every activation, including sub-tasks that never use them — kills the second level's whole purpose.

example.code:
## Deploying to faion-net
For the full deploy procedure, Read `references/deploy-runbook.md`. For the rollback procedure, Read `references/rollback.md`.
example.note: Body stays small; only the relevant runbook loads on demand.

example.code:
## Deploying to faion-net
```bash # 850 lines of deploy steps inlined here ... ```
example.note: Every activation pays the 850-line tax even when the user just asked for status.

template.purpose: Working frontmatter with all required fields and a trigger-rich description.
Minimal SKILL.md skeleton showing how to defer to references/ and scripts/.

see-also.link: Complete Guide to Building Skills for Claude (PDF).
Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills (Anthropic engineering blog).
